short_intro: Where science meets strategic IP decisions.
name: About DeepTech
opening_paragraph: Meletis Mexis, PhD, is the founder of TechnoGenesis. A physicist turned patent information professional, he combines ten years of scientific research in quantum optoelectronics with eight years of industrial IP experience. Clients get direct access to a physicist who understands both device physics and patent claim language.
opening_keyline: Clients get direct access to a physicist who understands both device physics and patent claim language.
career_timeline_subline: A present-to-past view of leadership, certification, and deep-tech patent expertise.

career_timeline.highlights: Deep-tech patent search and analysis. Scientific collaboration with universities.
Certified in International Patent Law — CIPA (UK).
Physics and mechanics IP portfolio management. Weekly patent committee reporting.
Led patent analyst team. Patentability, FTO, landscaping, and patent monitoring for vehicle cockpit innovation.
Qualified Patent Information Professional — international certification.
Deep-UV micro-lasers, GaN-based optical resonators, and exciton-polariton ZnO lasers. 21 publications.

credentials_subline: Governance, certifications, publications, and peer recognition that support high-stakes IP advisory work.
credentials: Certified in International Patent Law — PEB FC3, CIPA (UK) (since 2026)
Member, CFIB — Francophone Patent Information Experts’ Group (since 2021)
21 peer-reviewed publications and 10 international conference presentations
intro_text: With a dual background in quantum physics, photonics, and intellectual property, I help companies transform complex technologies into strong, defensible IP assets.

quick_facts: Reviewer for Applied Physics Letters since 2015
professional_highlights_subline: Key industry roles and contributions across patent strategy, analysis, and deep-tech innovation.
professional_highlights: CNRS researcher in photonics and semiconductor physics
scientific_achievements_subline: Selected breakthroughs and peer-reviewed contributions in photonics and semiconductor innovation.
scientific_achievements: GaN optical resonators (Opt. Lett 2011, Appl. Phys. Lett 2011)
